title: Oprah Winfrey
text:
American media personality and proprietor (born 1954)
"Oprah" redirects here; not to be confused with Orpah or Opera .
For the talk show, see *The Oprah Winfrey Show* . For the painting by Shawn Michael Warren, see Oprah Winfrey (painting)") .

Section Title: Early life > Influence > "The Oprah Effect"
Content:
<sup>[[ 203 ]]()</sup> In *Reading with Oprah: The Book Club that Changed America* (2005), Kathleen Rooney describes Winfrey as "a serious American intellectual who pioneered the use of electronic media, specifically television and the Internet, to take reading – a decidedly non-technological and highly individual act – and highlight its social elements and uses in such a way to motivate millions of erstwhile non-readers to pick up books."

Content:

*[Watching Oprah](https://nmaahc.si.edu/explore/exhibitions/watchingoprah)* ( [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20210417045044/https://nmaahc.si.edu/explore/exhibitions/watchingoprah) April 17, 2021, at the Wayback Machine )—Smithsonian exhibition on the *Oprah Show* and Winfrey

**Oprah Gail Winfrey** ( / ˈ oʊ p r ə / ; born **Orpah Gail Winfrey** ; <sup>[[ a ]]()</sup> January 29, 1954) is an American talk show host , television producer, actress, author, and media proprietor . She is best known for her talk show, *The Oprah Winfrey Show* , broadcast from Chicago , which ran in national syndication for 25 years, from 1986 to 2011. <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> Globally, she is the richest Black woman <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> and the wealthiest female celebrity . <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> Dubbed the " **Queen of All Media** ", <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> she was the richest African-American of the 20th century <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup> and was once the world's only Black billionaire. <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup> By 2007, she was often ranked as the most influential woman in the world. <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup>
